user.title: JD Smart (小京鱼) — Account credentials
user.description:
Just two things:
① **tgt** — login ticket (expires; must be the JD IoT '小京鱼' app's tgt);
② **device_id** — paste the device_id straight from your captured request/curl (a UUID like `b0b57721-…` is fine; it isn't signed and the server doesn't strictly validate it, so **no md5/android_id needed**).

Discovery, snapshots, control and the **full device model** all use the same gw/api.smart signing — **no jmafinger/pin/eid (rainbow) params needed**; model/version app params use defaults.

The full model (fan speeds/mode, numeric controls) is fetched **automatically** — no extra credentials. The same phone can hold multiple accounts (told apart by home).

data.tgt: tgt (login ticket, expires; JD IoT app)
data.device_id: device_id (paste from your captured request/curl; a UUID is fine)

houses.title: Select homes
houses.description: Choose which homes to pull devices from.

devices.title: Select devices
devices.description: Choose devices to add. Sensors are built from each device's streams.

error.need_identity: Fill device_id (paste it from your captured request/curl — a UUID is fine). If you only have android_id, set it under Advanced and it will be derived automatically.
error.cannot_connect: Discovery failed: {detail}. Usually an expired tgt or a network issue — make sure tgt is the latest value from the JD IoT app.

abort.already_configured: This account is already configured (matched by pin or home). Don't add the same account twice.
abort.cannot_connect: Discovery failed (network / risk control / credentials). Check tgt and retry.
abort.no_houses: No homes returned for this account.
abort.no_devices: No devices found in the selected homes.

credentials.title: Update credentials
credentials.description: Update these two (tgt expires most often): tgt login ticket and device_id (paste from your captured request/curl; a UUID is fine). The full model is fetched automatically — no extra credentials; android_id (alternative identity) lives under Advanced. After updating, run Re-discover if your devices changed.

data.tgt: tgt (login ticket, expires; JD IoT app)
data.device_id: device_id (paste from your captured request/curl; a UUID is fine)

settings.title: Advanced (all parameters)
settings.description: Discovery, snapshots, control and the full model all use the same gw/api.smart signing — only tgt + device identity (device_id or android_id; prefer device_id, paste the UUID). seg1/key/model/version are app-level constants you rarely need to touch. The rainbow params (pin/jmafinger/eid/device profile) are retired and no longer used.

data.tgt: tgt (login ticket, expires; JD IoT app)
data.device_id: device_id (preferred; paste from your captured request/curl; a UUID is fine)

streams.title: Edit sensors
streams.description: Pick a device to edit its streams.

edit_device.title: Streams of {device}

manual.title: Manual devices
manual.description: One per line: name|feed_id. device_id comes from the account identity (device_id, or md5(android_id)).

abort.cannot_connect: Discovery failed. Check tgt and retry.
abort.no_houses: No homes returned for this account.
abort.no_devices: No devices cached. Use Re-discover first.
abort.missing_device_config: Missing tgt or device identity (device_id/android_id). Open 'Update credentials' (or Advanced) and fill them first.

get_device_snapshot.name: Query device snapshot
get_device_snapshot.description: Call getDeviceSnapshot_v1 and return the device status snapshot.

device_id.name: device_id
device_id.description: Device id used in the query string.

feed_id.name: feed_id
feed_id.description: Device feed_id used in the body.

control_device.name: Control device
control_device.description: Call controlDevice_v1 to send commands; returns the updated streams.

feed_id.name: feed_id
feed_id.description: Target device feed_id (already selected in the integration).

stream_id.name: stream_id
stream_id.description: Stream id to control (single-write form, paired with value).

value.name: value
value.description: Code to write (single-write form). Switch 0/1; enum step code; number for numeric streams.

command.name: command (batch)
command.description: Raw command array to write multiple streams at once: [{stream_id, current_value}, ...].

get_device_model.name: Query device model (diagnostic)
get_device_model.description: Fetch the device's gw getDeviceDetails model; returns raw response + parsed model + control routing. Use it to debug 'why only a Power switch' — a control_map missing Mode/Wind/oscillation means getDeviceDetails returned no model at runtime and it silently fell back to card_meta (whose card_control only marks Power controllable).

feed_id.name: feed_id
feed_id.description: Target device feed_id (already selected in the integration).
